pattern: A stack models the robot's holding string; pop a held character to paper only while nothing smaller remains to come, using a suffix-minimum to decide.
hints: The robot's temporary string behaves like a stack: characters go in from the front of s and come out from the top to paper.
You should only write the top of the stack when no smaller character is still waiting in the unread part of s.
Precompute, for each position, the minimum character in the remaining suffix; push s left to right, and whenever the stack top is <= that suffix minimum, pop it to the output.

edge_inputs.description: smallest must wait under larger held letters
descending must reverse via the stack
duplicates of the minimum scattered through s

stress.description: random lowercase strings over a small alphabet to force frequent stack decisions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    alpha = 'abcde'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
stress.note: narrow alphabet creates many ties so suffix-minimum logic is exercised
